input.background: We propose to draw boxplots for the air sample data presented in prior illustrations (see Table 4.2).
input.question: Draw a boxplot for site 1 data: 22, 24, 28, 32, 36, 38, 42, 68.

output.answer: 1. The five-point summary is: 22, 26, 34, 40, 68. Therefore, the box extends from 26 to 40. A line for the median is drawn in the box at 34. 2. IQR=40-26=14. Therefore, Fence Lower=26-(1.5)(14)=5 and Fence Upper=40+(1.5)(14)=61. 3. There are no values below the lower fence. There is one value (68) above the upper fence. The upper outside value of 68 is plotted as a separate point above the box. 4. The smallest value still inside the fence (lower inside value) is 22. A whisker is drawn from Q1 to this inside value. The upper inside value is 42. A whisker is drawn from Q3 to this inside value.

site_1_data.description: Air sample data from site 1 to be used for creating a boxplot.
